title: U2 Reemerges with Anticipated EP Easter Lily: A Pitchfork Exclusive Review and Premiere
body:
In a remarkable display of creative momentum, Irish rock icons U2 have dropped their second EP of 2026, Easter Lily, just a month after the release of their previous offering, Days of Ash. The six-track EP marks the band's latest collaboration with legendary producer, Brian Eno, whose signature experimental approach is sure to inject a new layer of depth into U2's signature sound.

Easter Lily is the latest installment in U2's ongoing exploration of new sounds and themes. The band has been on a roll since the release of Songs of Surrender, their 2023 album of reworked classics, and the momentum has continued unabated with Days of Ash. The new EP's focus on the symbolism and spirituality of Easter adds a new layer of meaning to U2's music, and the collaboration with Eno promises to elevate the band's already impressive sonic landscape.

Brian Eno's involvement is a significant development for U2 fans, given his reputation as a master of ambient textures and sonic experimentation. Eno has worked with a wide range of artists over the years, from Talking Heads to Coldplay, and his unique approach to production has helped shape the sound of countless albums. His collaboration with U2 on Easter Lily is likely to yield some truly innovative results, and fans are eagerly anticipating the opportunity to hear the band's music pushed to new and exciting places.

The release of Easter Lily also underscores U2's commitment to exploring new themes and ideas in their music. The band has long been associated with exploring the intersection of faith, politics, and personal experience, and the symbolism of Easter offers a rich seam of material to mine. By releasing two EPs in quick succession, U2 is demonstrating a willingness to experiment and take risks, pushing the boundaries of what their music can do and say.

In a broader context, the release of Easter Lily highlights the ongoing relevance and appeal of U2's music. Despite being active for over four decades, the band remains a major force in the music world, with a loyal fan base and a continued ability to craft music that resonates with listeners around the world. As they continue to explore new sounds and themes, U2 is ensuring that their music remains fresh and exciting, even for fans who have been following them for years.

Looking ahead, the release of Easter Lily sets the stage for an exciting year of music from U2. With their continued collaboration with Brian Eno and their commitment to pushing the boundaries of their sound, the band is poised to deliver some truly innovative and compelling music. As fans eagerly await their next move, one thing is clear: U2 remains a vital and creative force in the music world, and Easter Lily is just the latest chapter in their ongoing journey.
